intent: Follow up after a client meeting to provide additional resources and next steps.
facts: Meeting was on June 15 regarding Q3 marketing strategy.
Client requested case studies on social media ROI.
We promised to share a draft content calendar by June 20.
reference:
Subject: Follow-up & Next Steps – Q3 Marketing Strategy

Dear [Client],

Thank you again for the productive meeting on June 15 regarding your Q3 marketing strategy. As promised, I’ve attached two case studies that illustrate the ROI of social media campaigns in your industry.

Additionally, I am pleased to share our draft content calendar, which we will finalise by June 20 as agreed. Please let me know if you have any questions or would like to adjust the timeline.

I look forward to continuing our collaboration.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

intent: Request for detailed proposal from a potential vendor.
facts: We need a proposal for a custom CRM implementation.
Deadline for submission is July 5.
Must include pricing, timeline, and references.
reference:
Subject: Request for Proposal – CRM Implementation

Dear [Vendor],

We are seeking a proposal for the implementation of a custom CRM system tailored to our business processes. Please provide a comprehensive document that includes:

- Detailed pricing structure
- Project timeline with milestones
- At least three client references from similar projects

Kindly submit your proposal by July 5. Should you require any additional information, do not hesitate to contact me.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]

intent: Thank a colleague for exceptional support on a project.
facts: They stayed late twice to help meet deadlines.
Their expertise in data analysis was invaluable.
The project was delivered successfully ahead of schedule.
reference:
Subject: Thank You for Your Outstanding Support

Dear [Colleague],

I want to express my sincere gratitude for your exceptional support on the recent project. Your willingness to stay late twice really made the difference, and your deep expertise in data analysis turned complex challenges into clear insights.

Thanks to you, we not only met the deadline but delivered ahead of schedule – a fantastic outcome for the whole team.

I truly appreciate your dedication and look forward to working together again.

Warm regards,
[Your Name]

intent: Apologise to a client for a delayed delivery.
facts: The shipment was delayed by 5 days due to a supplier issue.
We have expedited the new shipping.
We offer a 10% discount on their next order as compensation.
reference:
Subject: Apology for Delivery Delay

Dear [Client],

I am writing to sincerely apologise for the delay in delivering your order, which is now 5 days later than promised. This was caused by an unforeseen issue with our supplier, and we deeply regret the inconvenience this may have caused you.

We have already expedited the new shipment, and you will receive tracking details by end of day. To make up for the delay, we are pleased to offer a 10% discount on your next order – simply mention this email when placing it.

We value your business and are taking steps to prevent this from happening again.

Please accept our apologies once more.

Yours faithfully,
[Your Name]

intent: Provide a status update on a software development project.
facts: Backend API is 80% complete.
Frontend integration will start next week.
Testing phase is scheduled to begin on July 10.
reference:
Subject: Project Update – Development Status

Dear Team,

I am pleased to share that we are making steady progress on the project. The backend API is now 80% complete, and we are on track for a smooth transition. Starting next week, we will begin the frontend integration, which is expected to take about five days.

Testing is scheduled to commence on July 10, as per the original plan.

Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns.

Best,
[Your Name]

intent: Reschedule a meeting due to a conflict.
facts: Original meeting was set for June 22 at 10 AM.
The presenter has a family emergency.
Proposed new time is June 24 at 2 PM.
reference:
Subject: Meeting Reschedule – New Date & Time

Dear All,

I hope this message finds you well. Due to an unexpected family emergency, our presenter will not be able to join the meeting originally scheduled for June 22 at 10 AM. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.

We have tentatively rescheduled the meeting for June 24 at 2 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you; otherwise, we can find an alternative.

Thank you for your understanding.

Kind regards,
[Your Name]

intent: Request feedback from a client on a recent deliverable.
facts: We submitted the final report on June 18.
The report covers market analysis and recommendations.
We would like feedback by June 30 to incorporate into the final version.
reference:
Subject: Request for Your Feedback – Final Report

Dear [Client],

We hope you’ve had a chance to review the final report we submitted on June 18. The report provides a comprehensive market analysis and a set of actionable recommendations tailored to your goals.

We would greatly appreciate your feedback on the content and structure. If you could share your thoughts by June 30, we can incorporate any adjustments before the final publication.

Please feel free to reach out with any questions.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

intent: Introduce a new team member to a client.
facts: New team member is Jane Doe, Senior Consultant.
She has 10 years of experience in the same industry.
She will be the primary point of contact going forward.
reference:
Subject: Introducing Jane Doe – Your New Primary Contact

Dear [Client],

I am delighted to introduce Jane Doe, who is joining our team as Senior Consultant. Jane brings over 10 years of experience in your industry and has a proven track record of delivering exceptional results.

Going forward, Jane will be your primary point of contact for all project matters. I am confident that her expertise will add great value to our collaboration, and I know you’ll enjoy working with her.

Please join me in welcoming Jane – she will be reaching out shortly to introduce herself personally.

Warmly,
[Your Name]

intent: Escalate a critical issue to a higher manager for urgent resolution.
facts: The production server is down since 2 PM.
The internal team has been unable to fix it.
We need approval to engage an external expert immediately.
reference:
Subject: URGENT – Production Server Outage

Dear [Manager],

I am writing to escalate a critical issue: the production server has been down since 2 PM today. Our internal team has exhausted all troubleshooting steps without success.

We urgently need your approval to engage an external expert who can resolve this quickly. Every minute of downtime affects our clients and revenue.

Please provide your authorisation as soon as possible – I am available on [phone] to discuss further.

Thank you,
[Your Name]

intent: Request an extension for a project deadline.
facts: The deadline is June 30.
We need two additional weeks because of scope creep.
We can deliver a partial version by the original deadline if needed.
reference:
Subject: Request for Deadline Extension

Dear [Client],

I hope you are well. I am writing to request a two‑week extension on the current project deadline (June 30). Due to recently added scope items, we need the extra time to ensure the final deliverable meets the high standards we both expect.

We understand the importance of the deadline and can deliver a functional partial version by June 30 if that would be helpful. However, to complete all features and testing thoroughly, we propose a new deadline of July 14.

Please let us know if this works for you – we are happy to discuss alternative arrangements.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
